milestones.title: M001: RVC Web App
milestones.vision: A polished Next.js + FastAPI application that wraps the RVC v2 pipeline into three clean features: a voice sample library, a fine-tuning training pipeline with live log streaming, and a realtime voice conversion loop with live waveforms — all orchestrated from a browser tab, output routed through BlackHole 2ch.
success_criteria: User can upload a voice sample, see it listed in the library, and select it for training.
User can trigger fine-tuning and watch preprocessing → feature extraction → training logs stream live in the browser.
User can start a realtime VC session selecting any input/output device; their voice is converted and plays on the chosen output device.
Input and output waveforms update live in the browser without UI jank (rendering in Web Worker).
`./scripts/start.sh` starts both services; `./scripts/stop.sh` stops them cleanly.
The base pretrained v2 model is never modified; one fine-tuned copy is maintained in `assets/weights/`.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown:
## Boundary Map

### S01 → S02

Produces:
- `GET /api/profiles` → `[{id, name, status, created_at, sample_path}]`
- `POST /api/profiles` → create profile with uploaded audio, returns `{id, name, status}`
- `DELETE /api/profiles/:id` → removes profile + files
- `GET /api/profiles/:id` → single profile detail
- SQLite schema: `profiles(id TEXT PK, name TEXT, status TEXT, sample_path TEXT, model_path TEXT, index_path TEXT, created_at TEXT)`
- `data/samples/<profile_id>/` directory convention for audio storage
- FastAPI app module: `backend/app/main.py` with CORS configured for `localhost:3000`
- Env/config pattern: `RVC_ROOT` env var pointing to `Retrieval-based-Voice-Conversion-WebUI/`

Consumes:
- nothing (first slice)

### S01 → S03

Produces (same as S01 → S02, plus):
- `GET /api/devices` → `[{id, name, is_input, is_output}]` (sounddevice query)
- Profile `status` field values: `untrained | training | trained | failed`

### S02 → S03

Produces:
- `POST /api/training/start` body `{profile_id}` → `{job_id}` (triggers training subprocess chain)
- `POST /api/training/cancel` body `{profile_id}` → kills subprocess
- `GET /api/training/status/:profile_id` → `{phase, progress_pct, status}`
- `WS /ws/training/:profile_id` → streams `{type: "log"|"phase"|"done"|"error", message, phase}` JSON lines
- `assets/weights/rvc_finetune_active.pth` — fine-tuned model file produced by training
- `logs/<profile_id>/added_IVF*_Flat*.index` — FAISS index file produced by training
- Profile status updated to `trained` in SQLite when training completes

### S03 → S04

Produces:
- `POST /api/realtime/start` body `{profile_id, input_device_id, output_device_id, pitch, index_rate, protect}` → `{session_id}`
- `POST /api/realtime/stop` body `{session_id}` → stops audio loop
- `POST /api/realtime/params` body `{session_id, pitch?, index_rate?, protect?}` → hot-updates running session params
- `WS /ws/realtime/:session_id` → streams `{type: "waveform_in"|"waveform_out", samples: Float32Array as base64, sr: number}` at ~20fps
- `GET /api/realtime/status` → `{active: bool, session_id, profile_id, input_device, output_device}`
- `AudioProcessor` interface: `process(audio: np.ndarray, sample_rate: int) -> np.ndarray` — stageable pipeline

### S04 → (end)

Produces:
- `scripts/start.sh` — starts backend (conda run -n rvc uvicorn) + frontend (pnpm dev), writes PIDs
- `scripts/stop.sh` — kills by PID files
- Next.js pages: `/` (Library tab), `/training` (Training tab), `/realtime` (Realtime tab) — or single page with tab navigation
- In-app BlackHole setup guide component

milestones.title: Training Quality Enhancements: TPRLS, KL Annealing, AdamSPD
milestones.vision: Expose three training quality levers — TPRLS adversarial loss, cyclic KL annealing, and AdamSPD optimizer — through the full stack from training UI to subprocess, improving voice cloning convergence quality without changing the inference pipeline.
success_criteria: TPRLS and LSGAN selectable per training run; discriminator and generator both use the chosen loss; default remains LSGAN for backward compat
Cyclic KL annealing toggle in UI; when enabled, kl_beta cycles 0→1 over configurable epoch window rather than fixed β=1
AdamW and AdamSPD selectable per training run; AdamSPD applies stiffness penalty toward pretrain weights; default remains AdamW
All three settings flow through DB (migration), API (Pydantic), config.json patch, CLI, and train.py loss computation
Frontend UI renders all three controls cleanly within the existing training config section
Existing profiles and runs with no new settings behave identically (all defaults match prior behaviour)

key_risks.risk: AdamSPD stiffness penalty requires a copy of pretrain weights at optimizer init — memory overhead on MPS with large models
key_risks.whyItMatters: Could OOM on 8GB M1 with bs=8

key_risks.risk: TPRLS median computation is O(n) per discriminator head — small but nonzero per-step overhead
KL annealing with short cycle duration can cause posterior collapse oscillation
key_risks.whyItMatters: Bad UX if default cycle is too short

proof_strategy.whatWillBeProven: Start a training run with each non-default option; verify subprocess log confirms the chosen loss/optimizer is active
Training starts and runs 2 epochs without OOM on MPS with bs=8
Existing profile with no new columns starts training normally with LSGAN + AdamW + no annealing

milestones.verification_contract: All 3 features configurable end-to-end; train.py logs confirm chosen loss type and optimizer at startup; existing defaults unchanged.
milestones.verification_integration: Frontend POST body contains new fields; API accepts them; config.json written with correct values; train.py reads and uses them.
milestones.verification_operational: Training runs complete without error for all combinations: LSGAN+AdamW (baseline), TPRLS+AdamW, LSGAN+AdamSPD, KL annealing on/off.
milestones.verification_uat: User can select loss type, toggle KL annealing, select optimizer from the training config UI; settings are visible before starting training.
definition_of_done: losses.py: TPRLS disc and gen loss functions added
db.py: adv_loss, kl_anneal, kl_anneal_epochs, optimizer columns migrated
training.py: _write_config and _run_pipeline accept and propagate new params
routers/training.py: StartTrainingRequest extended; /start reads new fields
train.py: loss branching on hps.train.adv_loss; KL beta schedule; optimizer branching
Frontend: adv_loss selector, KL anneal toggle+cycle input, optimizer selector rendered and wired to handleStart
Python syntax check passes on all modified .py files
TypeScript type check passes on training page
milestones.requirement_coverage: Covers training quality improvement requirements identified in TRAINING_COMPARISON.md analysis.
milestones.boundary_map_markdown: `train.py` owns loss computation and optimizer init. `_write_config` owns config.json patching. `losses.py` owns loss function implementations. `adamspd.py` is a new self-contained module. Frontend owns UI state and POST body. DB owns persistence of per-profile settings.

slices.title: Backend Foundation & Voice Sample Library
slices.demo: FastAPI server runs from `rvc-web/`, SQLite schema initialized, REST API for creating/listing/deleting voice profiles is live, file upload stores audio to disk and records to DB — verified by curl.
slices.goal: FastAPI server runs from `rvc-web/`, SQLite schema is initialized, REST API for creating/listing/getting/deleting voice profiles is live, file upload stores audio to disk and records to the DB, and `GET /api/devices` returns system audio devices — all verified by `pytest` contract tests and `curl`.

slices.title: Training Pipeline with Live Log Streaming
slices.demo: User can POST to start training for a profile, watch preprocess → feature extract → train logs stream via WebSocket in a test client, and find a real `.pth` in `assets/weights/` when training completes.
slices.goal: POST to start training for a profile, watch preprocess → feature extract → train logs stream via WebSocket in a test client, and find a real `.pth` in `assets/weights/` when training completes.

slices.title: Realtime Voice Conversion & Waveform Visualization
slices.demo: User opens the Realtime tab in the browser, selects MacBook Pro Microphone → BlackHole 2ch, starts session, speaks, and hears their converted voice on BlackHole output; input/output waveforms animate live in the browser.
slices.goal: A running FastAPI backend endpoint that starts a realtime RVC audio loop reading from the MacBook Pro Microphone and writing converted audio to BlackHole 2ch, while streaming input/output waveform data over WebSocket at ~20fps; all five API endpoints verified by passing contract tests; a minimal Realtime tab in the Next.js frontend renders live waveform canvases via a Web Worker.

slices.title: Next.js Frontend Polish & Service Scripts
slices.demo: All three features are accessible through a polished, responsive Next.js UI; `./scripts/start.sh` starts everything and opens the browser; `./scripts/stop.sh` stops cleanly; in-app BlackHole setup guide is visible.
slices.goal: Complete the Next.js UI with Library and Training tabs alongside the existing Realtime tab; write `scripts/start.sh` / `scripts/stop.sh`; add an in-app BlackHole setup guide. Every owned requirement (R007, R008) is proven. R001 and R002 gain their front-end delivery surface.

# T01: Added TPRLS disc/gen loss functions to losses.py and AdamSPD optimizer module

**Added TPRLS disc/gen loss functions to losses.py and AdamSPD optimizer module**

## What Happened

Added discriminator_tprls_loss and generator_tprls_loss to losses.py using median-centering approach from fork-4 reference. Created adamspd.py with a clean AdamSPD implementation that snapshots pretrain anchor weights and applies selective projection decay only when the gradient would push parameters further from the anchor.

## Verification

python3 -m py_compile on both files passes

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `python3 -m py_compile backend/rvc/infer/lib/train/losses.py && python3 -m py_compile backend/rvc/infer/lib/train/adamspd.py` | 0 | ✅ pass | 120ms |

## Deviations

None.

## Known Issues

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- `backend/rvc/infer/lib/train/losses.py`
- `backend/rvc/infer/lib/train/adamspd.py`

# T05: Wired train.py: adv_loss branching, KL beta schedule, AdamSPD optimizer init

**Wired train.py: adv_loss branching, KL beta schedule, AdamSPD optimizer init**

## What Happened

Imported TPRLS functions. Added optimizer branching at init (AdamW vs AdamSPD with anchor snapshot). Added discriminator loss branching (lsgan vs tprls). Added cyclic KL beta schedule. Added generator loss branching. Startup log line confirms chosen adv_loss and optimizer.

## Verification

py_compile passes

## Verification Evidence

| # | Command | Exit Code | Verdict | Duration |
|---|---------|-----------|---------|----------|
| 1 | `python3 -m py_compile backend/rvc/infer/modules/train/train.py` | 0 | ✅ pass | 200ms |

## Deviations

TPRLS generator loss uses y_d_hat_r (real outputs) in addition to y_d_hat_g since TPRLS is a paired relative loss — both are needed. Moved _adv_loss assignment outside the inner autocast block so it's shared between disc and gen sections.

## Known Issues

None.

## Files Created/Modified

- `backend/rvc/infer/modules/train/train.py`
tasks.description:
1. In train_and_evaluate: branch discriminator_loss / generator_loss on hps.train.adv_loss ('lsgan'|'tprls')
2. Import tprls functions from losses.py
3. Compute kl_beta: if hps.train.kl_anneal: beta = cyclic cosine over kl_anneal_epochs, else 1.0
4. Apply kl_beta: loss_kl = kl_loss(...) * hps.train.c_kl * kl_beta
5. In optimizer init block: branch on hps.train.optimizer ('adamw'|'adamspd'); for adamspd import and init AdamSPD with pretrain anchor
6. Log chosen adv_loss and optimizer at startup
tasks.verify: python3 -m py_compile backend/rvc/infer/modules/train/train.py
expected_output: train.py uses hps.train.adv_loss to pick loss fn

# T01: Added adv_loss selector, KL anneal toggle, optimizer selector to training page UI

**Added adv_loss selector, KL anneal toggle, optimizer selector to training page UI**

## What Happened

Added 4 state variables (advLoss, klAnneal, klAnnealEpochs, optimizer). Extended handleStart POST body with all 4. Added 3 new UI sections after the Training Objective section: Adversarial Loss tile selector (LSGAN/TPRLS), KL Annealing toggle with cycle epochs slider, Optimizer tile selector (AdamW/AdamSPD). 1. Add state: advLoss ('lsgan'|'tprls'), klAnneal (bool), klAnnealEpochs (number), optimizer ('adamw'|'adamspd')
2. Add adv_loss, kl_anneal, kl_anneal_epochs, optimizer to handleStart POST body
3. Render in training config section (after overtraining controls, before Start button):

   - 'KL Annealing' row: toggle switch; when on show cycle epochs number input (default 40)
   - 'Optimizer' row: two tile buttons AdamW / AdamSPD with brief tooltip

handleStart POST body includes 4 new fields

decisions.choice: SQLite via aiosqlite
decisions.rationale: PostgreSQL not installed; zero-setup; single-user local tool; trivially swappable later

decisions.choice: FastAPI + uvicorn
decisions.rationale: Already in rvc conda env; async support for WebSocket log streaming; minimal overhead

decisions.choice: Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind
decisions.rationale: User specified Next.js; App Router is current standard

decisions.choice: sounddevice (Python) loop → BlackHole 2ch output
decisions.rationale: User confirmed backend-only audio processing; browser only controls start/stop and displays waveforms

decisions.choice: Web Worker + OffscreenCanvas
decisions.rationale: Keeps main thread free; explicit user requirement

decisions.choice: rtrvc.py RVC class directly (no copy)
decisions.rationale: Upstream code is well-maintained; wrapping avoids drift; run from RVC_ROOT working dir

decisions.choice: Single copy at assets/weights/rvc_finetune_active.pth
decisions.rationale: User explicitly requested one copy; overwrite on re-train. Base pretrained models are never touched.
decisions.revisable: Yes — expand to per-profile slots in M002

decisions.choice: List of AudioProcessor objects (process: np.ndarray → np.ndarray)
decisions.rationale: Explicit user request for future effects extensibility; simple enough to not over-engineer now
decisions.revisable: Yes — could grow to a graph if needed

decisions.choice: Launch preprocess.py / extract_feature_print.py / train.py as subprocess, stream stdout via async queue
decisions.rationale: Matches how web.py orchestrates training; log lines available for WebSocket streaming

decisions.choice: Try MPS first; fallback to CPU if MPS raises on training ops
decisions.rationale: MPS available (torch 2.10, mps.is_available=True) but training ops may be unsupported; inference uses MPS

decisions.choice: conda run -n rvc uvicorn backend.app.main:app for backend; pnpm dev for frontend
decisions.rationale: rvc conda env must be active for all Python deps; conda run activates inline

decisions.choice: rvc-web/ contains backend/ and frontend/ at root alongside Retrieval-based-Voice-Conversion-WebUI/
decisions.rationale: Keeps RVC repo self-contained; RVC_ROOT env var bridges backend to RVC code

decisions.choice: aiosqlite per-request connections via async context manager (`async with aiosqlite.connect(DB_PATH)`)
decisions.rationale: Simplest safe pattern for single-user local tool; avoids shared-connection locking complexity
decisions.revisable: Yes — switch to connection pool if concurrency becomes relevant

decisions.choice: FastAPI TestClient with temp SQLite DB via env var override; no running server needed for contract tests
decisions.rationale: httpx TestClient is already installed; per-test temp DB keeps tests hermetic; avoids port conflicts

decisions.choice: `uuid.uuid4().hex` (32 hex chars, no dashes)
decisions.rationale: Safe for use as filesystem directory name on all platforms; unambiguous primary key

decisions.choice: JSON startup log line + `/health` endpoint expose DB path and RVC_ROOT on every boot
decisions.rationale: Enables future agents to confirm configuration without reading env files; `/health` is cheap liveness check

decisions.choice: In-memory `Dict[str, TrainingJob]` keyed by `profile_id`; single active job at a time
decisions.rationale: Single-user local tool; no persistence of in-flight state needed; simple and sufficient
decisions.revisable: Yes — extend to per-job persistence if multi-user or restart-recovery needed

decisions.choice: `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` with `stdout=PIPE, stderr=STDOUT` streaming lines into `asyncio.Queue`
decisions.rationale: Preprocess/extract scripts print to stdout; non-blocking line streaming integrates cleanly with asyncio queue

decisions.choice: Poll `train.log` file via `aiofiles` every 200ms; wait up to 10s for file creation
decisions.rationale: train.py writes logs only to file via FileHandler — no stdout; file polling is the only viable streaming approach

decisions.choice: In-process via `run_in_executor(None, _build_index)` adapting `train_index()` logic from `web.py`
decisions.rationale: CPU-bound; no subprocess needed; keeps index path predictable; avoids spawning another subprocess just for Python logic

decisions.choice: `starlette.testclient.TestClient.websocket_connect()` for WebSocket tests; `httpx` async client for REST tests
decisions.rationale: `httpx` does not support WebSocket; starlette TestClient synchronous API works cleanly for WS assertions

decisions.choice: `exp_name = "rvc_finetune_active"` — single global training slot; model lands at `assets/weights/rvc_finetune_active.pth`
decisions.rationale: D007 decision: one fine-tuned copy per D007; fixed name ensures predictable path for S03 to consume
decisions.revisable: Yes — expand to per-profile in M002 per D007

decisions.choice: Separate `ws_router = APIRouter()` (no prefix) for WebSocket endpoint; REST endpoints on `router = APIRouter(prefix='/api/training')`
decisions.rationale: A router with prefix '/api/training' would make WS route '/api/training/ws/training/{id}' — wrong URL. Two routers, both registered in main.py, produce correct paths

decisions.choice: Added `rmvpe_root` to `train_env` in `training.py` before launching train subprocess
decisions.rationale: `save_small_model` in `process_ckpt.py` calls `model_hash_ckpt` → `Pipeline.__init__` which reads `os.environ["rmvpe_root"]`; missing env caused `KeyError` and `.pth` save failure

decisions.choice: Patched `process_ckpt.py:save_small_model` to catch `model_hash_ckpt` failure and proceed without hash/id metadata
decisions.rationale: PyTorch 2.6 changed `torch.load` default to `weights_only=True`; fairseq's `load_checkpoint_to_cpu` now raises `UnpicklingError` for HuBERT globals; hash is DRM metadata only — model is fully functional without it
decisions.revisable: Yes — upstream fairseq fix would be cleaner if maintained

decisions.choice: `pytest.ini` at repo root: `asyncio_mode=strict`, `integration` mark registered
decisions.rationale: No pytest config existed; `PytestUnknownMarkWarning` on `integration` mark; centralises pytest configuration

decisions.choice: In-memory `Dict[str, RealtimeSession]` keyed by `session_id`; one active session at a time; mirrors D017 TrainingManager pattern
decisions.rationale: Single-user local tool; no persistence of in-flight audio sessions needed; `active_session()` guard enforces one-at-a-time
decisions.revisable: Yes — extend to concurrent sessions if multi-user deployment added

decisions.choice: Separate `sd.InputStream` + `sd.OutputStream` (not `sd.Stream`) with native rates (44100 Hz in, 48000 Hz out)
decisions.rationale: MacBook Pro Mic natively 44100 Hz; BlackHole natively 48000 Hz; separate streams avoid mixed-rate `sd.Stream` complexity

decisions.choice: Dedicated `threading.Thread` (daemon=True) for audio loop; `collections.deque(maxlen=40)` for waveform data handoff to asyncio WebSocket
decisions.rationale: sounddevice callbacks fire in C threads; asyncio cannot be used directly; thread-safe deque + asyncio polling at 50ms is the correct bridge

decisions.choice: asyncio task polls `_waveform_deque` every 50ms (~20fps); `deque.popleft()` is thread-safe for single-producer single-consumer
decisions.rationale: Avoids `call_soon_threadsafe` complexity; 50ms poll lag is imperceptible at 20fps target
decisions.revisable: Yes — switch to `call_soon_threadsafe` if latency reduction matters

decisions.decision: Block size fixed at 9600 samples (200ms at 48kHz)
decisions.choice: `block_48k=9600`; ~37ms steady-state inference latency → 0.19× realtime ratio on MPS
decisions.rationale: Research confirmed 5.4× headroom at 200ms; JIT warmup ~1.9s happens once per session; can reduce to 4800 (100ms) if latency is perceptible
decisions.revisable: Yes — revisit in S03/T04 integration test

decisions.choice: Subsample waveform to ~800 points per message: `decimation = block_48k // 800`; encode as base64 float32 bytes
decisions.rationale: 9600 → 800 points = 12× decimation; 800 points sufficient for visual waveform; smaller message = faster WebSocket delivery

decisions.choice: Router calls `result = manager.start_session(...); session = await result if asyncio.iscoroutine(result) else result`
decisions.rationale: Test mock_manager uses `MagicMock` (sync), not `AsyncMock`; unconditional `await` on MagicMock raises TypeError; guard lets both sync mock and real async manager work without touching test code
decisions.revisable: Yes — if manager is ever replaced, ensure new impl is consistently async

decisions.choice: Router does not check RVC_ROOT independently; passes it as optional kwarg to manager.start_session
decisions.rationale: DB-level validation (404/400/409) must fire before env-var gate; test contracts expect these responses without RVC_ROOT set; real manager handles absent RVC_ROOT internally

decisions.decision: Device API schema uses is_input/is_output booleans
decisions.choice: `/api/devices` returns `{id, name, is_input: bool, is_output: bool}` — frontend filters inputs/outputs with `d.is_input` and `d.is_output`
decisions.rationale: S01 contract in `devices.py` defines this schema; task plan incorrectly referenced a `kind` field; corrected in frontend implementation

decisions.decision: Frontend bootstrapped fresh with Next.js 16 + App Router + Tailwind 4
decisions.choice: `pnpm create next-app frontend --typescript --tailwind --app` at project root
decisions.rationale: No frontend directory existed; PROJECT.md specified Next.js 14 App Router but pnpm create next-app defaults to current stable (16); App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind are correct per M001 architecture
decisions.revisable: Yes — upgrade notes if Next.js 16 breaking changes surface

decisions.decision: Realtime tab includes Voice Profile selector
decisions.choice: Frontend fetches `/api/profiles`, filters `status='trained'`, renders dropdown; shows empty-state message if none
decisions.rationale: Task plan hardcoded `profile_id: 'rvc_finetune_active'` as placeholder; real UI needs profile selection; selector is more correct and future-proof for M002 per-profile slots

decisions.decision: NavBar extracted as client component inside layout.tsx
decisions.choice: `NavBar` is `'use client'` (uses `usePathname`); `RootLayout` stays a Server Component
decisions.rationale: Next.js App Router requires `usePathname` in a Client Component; extracting NavBar avoids making the entire layout a client boundary

decisions.decision: Library page polls GET /api/realtime/status at 3s interval
decisions.choice: `setInterval` in `useEffect`; cleared on unmount
decisions.rationale: Provides low-cost session-active indicator on the Library page without a persistent WebSocket; 3s lag is imperceptible for a status badge
decisions.revisable: Yes — switch to WS push if real-time accuracy matters

decisions.decision: Training page shows all profiles (not just trained) in dropdown
decisions.choice: Profile dropdown on Training page uses all profiles regardless of status
decisions.rationale: User may want to re-train any profile; filtering to trained-only would prevent re-training; Library page already shows status — no need to duplicate filtering on Training

decisions.decision: WS_BASE constant separate from API constant in Training page
decisions.choice: `const WS_BASE = 'ws://localhost:8000'` alongside `const API = 'http://localhost:8000'`
decisions.rationale: WebSocket URLs require `ws://` scheme; deriving from API string by replace is fragile; explicit separate constant is clearer and safe

verification_evidence.command: python3 -m py_compile backend/rvc/infer/lib/train/losses.py && python3 -m py_compile backend/rvc/infer/lib/train/adamspd.py
